T_train.prompt: Explain how async/await works in JavaScript and why it's better than raw promises.
T_train.reward_def: y=1 if output (a) <=40% token compression vs natural English, (b) keeps technical terms async/await/promise/callback exact, (c) preserves causal/explanatory logic; y=0 otherwise

T_train.prompt: Could you please review my PR when you have time? I'd really value your feedback on the error handling approach.
T_train.reward_def: y=1 if output (a) drops pleasantries, (b) preserves the request intent (review PR + feedback on error handling), (c) doesn't trigger false Auto-Clarity Exception (this is NOT a security/irreversible action); y=0 if either over-compresses losing request intent, or false-triggers Auto-Clarity

T_val.prompt: Walk me through deploying this service step by step: build the binary, run tests, push the image, and deploy to production.
T_val.reward_def: y=1 if output (a) compresses pleasantries, (b) preserves all 4 steps in correct order, (c) handles the Auto-Clarity boundary correctly (multi-step deployment IS risky, but steps themselves can be caveman; deployment confirmation should NOT be caveman)
T_val.note: designed to expose Auto-Clarity Exception subjectivity — different agents may handle differently
